task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is visible with 'simple_model_rocket' shown in the title bar or the component tree.

task_completion.description: Integrate a new body tube with approximately 100mm length and 25.4mm outer diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new 'Body tube' is visible in the OpenRocket component tree. The configuration window for this tube should show a length of ~10 cm (100 mm) and an outer diameter of ~2.54 cm (25.4 mm).

task_completion.description: Add a 30-gram electronics mass component inside the new body tube.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Mass component' is visible in the component tree, nested under the newly added body tube. The configuration window for this mass component should show a mass of ~30 g (0.030 kg).

task_completion.description: Ensure the final design maintains a stability margin of at least 1.0 caliber.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenRocket window displays a 'Stability' value in the upper right corner (or flight performance section) that is >= 1.00 cal.
A 'Save As' dialog is visible saving to the 'rockets' folder as 'payload_integrated_rocket.ork', or the OpenRocket window title updates to 'payload_integrated_rocket.ork'.

task_completion.description: Document the added components, payload mass, final stability margin, and simulated apogee altitude in a report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'payload_report.txt' containing text that lists the added components (body tube, mass), the mass (~30g), the stability margin (>= 1.0), and an apogee altitude value in meters.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket graphical interface being interacted with (e.g., component configuration dialogs), rather than the agent directly editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent ran a flight simulation to obtain the apogee altitude.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket is visible with a completed simulation run showing the apogee, proving the reported apogee is genuine and not hallucinated.

integrity.description: The agent created the report using a text editor or standard terminal commands, reflecting actual values from the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into the text report match the values seen in the OpenRocket GUI screenshots (e.g., stability and apogee match exactly).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target modified rocket file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/payload_integrated_rocket.ork'. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/payload_report.txt'. The new body tube length should be approximately 100mm (acceptable range: 70mm - 130mm). The mass component should be approximately 30g (acceptable range: 20g - 50g). The final stability margin must be at least 1.0 caliber.
